  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 hover:text-text-100 Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy link to clipboard" title="Copy link to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="inline" style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="inline" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.623 9.648a.5.5 0 0 1 .648.281l1.008 2.559a4 4 0 0 1-7.363 3.122l-.08-.19-1.008-2.558a.5.5 0 0 1 .93-.367l1.008 2.558a3 3 0 1 0 5.583-2.198l-1.008-2.559a.5.5 0 0 1 .282-.648M8.9 7.208a.5.5 0 0 1 .649.283l1.833 4.652a.5.5 0 0 1-.931.366L8.619 7.857a.5.5 0 0 1 .281-.649M7.17 2.253a4 4 0 0 1 4.994 2.327l1.008 2.558a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366l-1.009-2.558a3 3 0 0 0-5.582 2.199L6.66 9.704a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366L4.72 7.51a4 4 0 0 1 2.255-5.187z"></path></svg></div></button></div><div>Capability-specific tips</div><div class="absolute top-0 -right-6 hidden lg:flex"></div></div></h2>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100"><div class="group relative pt-6 pb-2" id="improved-vision-capabilities"><div class="absolute hidden lg:flex lg:items-center lg:-ml-8 top-[calc(1.5rem+0.08em)]"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0
  can-focus
  select-none
  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:shadow-none
  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 hover:text-text-100 Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy link to clipboard" title="Copy link to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="inline" style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="inline" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.623 9.648a.5.5 0 0 1 .648.281l1.008 2.559a4 4 0 0 1-7.363 3.122l-.08-.19-1.008-2.558a.5.5 0 0 1 .93-.367l1.008 2.558a3 3 0 1 0 5.583-2.198l-1.008-2.559a.5.5 0 0 1 .282-.648M8.9 7.208a.5.5 0 0 1 .649.283l1.833 4.652a.5.5 0 0 1-.931.366L8.619 7.857a.5.5 0 0 1 .281-.649M7.17 2.253a4 4 0 0 1 4.994 2.327l1.008 2.558a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366l-1.009-2.558a3 3 0 0 0-5.582 2.199L6.66 9.704a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366L4.72 7.51a4 4 0 0 1 2.255-5.187z"></path></svg></div></button></div><div>Improved vision capabilities</div><div class="absolute top-0 -right-6 hidden lg:flex"></div></div></h3>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">Claude Opus 4.5 and Claude Opus 4.6 have improved vision capabilities compared to previous Claude models. They perform better on image processing and data extraction tasks, particularly when there are multiple images present in context. These improvements carry over to computer use, where the models can more reliably interpret screenshots and UI elements. You can also use these models to analyze videos by breaking them up into frames.</p>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">One technique that has proven effective to further boost performance is to give Claude a crop tool or <a class="inline-link" href="/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/overview">skill</a>. Testing has shown consistent uplift on image evaluations when Claude is able to &quot;zoom&quot; in on relevant regions of an image. Anthropic has created a <a class="inline-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://platform.claude.com/cookbook/multimodal-crop-tool">cookbook for the crop tool</a>.</p>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100"><div class="group relative pt-6 pb-2" id="frontend-design"><div class="absolute hidden lg:flex lg:items-center lg:-ml-8 top-[calc(1.5rem+0.08em)]"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0
  can-focus
  select-none
  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:shadow-none
  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 hover:text-text-100 Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy link to clipboard" title="Copy link to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="inline" style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="inline" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.623 9.648a.5.5 0 0 1 .648.281l1.008 2.559a4 4 0 0 1-7.363 3.122l-.08-.19-1.008-2.558a.5.5 0 0 1 .93-.367l1.008 2.558a3 3 0 1 0 5.583-2.198l-1.008-2.559a.5.5 0 0 1 .282-.648M8.9 7.208a.5.5 0 0 1 .649.283l1.833 4.652a.5.5 0 0 1-.931.366L8.619 7.857a.5.5 0 0 1 .281-.649M7.17 2.253a4 4 0 0 1 4.994 2.327l1.008 2.558a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366l-1.009-2.558a3 3 0 0 0-5.582 2.199L6.66 9.704a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366L4.72 7.51a4 4 0 0 1 2.255-5.187z"></path></svg></div></button></div><div>Frontend design</div><div class="absolute top-0 -right-6 hidden lg:flex"></div></div></h3>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">Claude Opus 4.5 and Claude Opus 4.6 excel at building complex, real-world web applications with strong frontend design. However, without guidance, models can default to generic patterns that create what users call the &quot;AI slop&quot; aesthetic. To create distinctive, creative frontends that surprise and delight:</p>
<div class="flex w-full rounded-lg px-4 py-3 gap-1 items-start text-sm border-0.5 my-6 border-border-200 bg-bg-200 callout"><div class="flex mr-2 shrink-0 pt-[1.375px] text-text-000"><div style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M12.5 17a.5.5 0 0 1 0 1h-5a.5.5 0 0 1 0-1zM10 2a6 6 0 0 1 4.081 10.398l-.242.213c-.512.427-.839.964-.839 1.513V15.5a.5.5 0 0 1-.5.5h-5a.5.5 0 0 1-.5-.5v-1.376c0-.48-.25-.951-.656-1.348l-.183-.165A6 6 0 0 1 10 2m0 1a5 5 0 0 0-3.2 8.843l.237.213c.537.523.963 1.234.963 2.068V15h1.5v-3.793L7.146 8.854l-.064-.079a.5.5 0 0 1 .693-.693l.079.064L10 10.293l2.146-2.147.079-.064a.5.5 0 0 1 .693.693l-.064.079-2.354 2.353V15H12v-.876c0-.953.557-1.746 1.2-2.281l.2-.178A5 5 0 0 0 10 3"></path></svg></div></div><div class="grow min-w-0 leading-relaxed [&amp;&gt;*:last-child]:mb-0 text-text-000 [&amp;_li]:before:text-text-000/60 [&amp;_blockquote]:border-l-text-000/60 [&amp;_code]:text-text-000 [&amp;_code]:bg-text-000/10"><p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">For a detailed guide on improving frontend design, see the blog post on <a class="inline-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://www.claude.com/blog/improving-frontend-design-through-skills">improving frontend design through skills</a>.</p></div></div>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">Here&#x27;s a system prompt snippet you can use to encourage better frontend design:</p>
<div class="group relative my-6 rounded-lg bg-bg-000 overflow-hidden border-0.5 border-border-300"><div class="group flex items-center justify-between border-b-0.5 border-border-300 px-4 py-1"><div class="text-xs py-1 flex items-center gap-2 text-text-500">Sample prompt for frontend aesthetics</div><div class="flex items-center gap-1"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0
  can-focus
  select-none
  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:shadow-none
  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy code"><div class="relative flex items-center justify-center"><div class="transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-100" style="width:14px;height:14px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-100" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M12.5 3A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 14 4.5V6h1.5A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 17 7.5v8a1.5 1.5 0 0 1-1.5 1.5h-8A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 6 15.5V14H4.5A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 3 12.5v-8A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 4.5 3zm1.5 9.5a1.5 1.5 0 0 1-1.5 1.5H7v1.5a.5.5 0 0 0 .5.5h8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5-.5v-8a.5.5 0 0 0-.5-.5H14zM4.5 4a.5.5 0 0 0-.5.5v8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5.5h8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5-.5v-8a.5.5 0 0 0-.5-.5z"></path></svg></div><div class="absolute transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-0" style="width:14px;height:14px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="absolute transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-0" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M15.188 5.11a.5.5 0 0 1 .752.626l-.056.084-7.5 9a.5.5 0 0 1-.738.033l-3.5-3.5-.064-.078a.501.501 0 0 1 .693-.693l.078.064 3.113 3.113 7.15-8.58z"></path></svg></div></div></button></div></div><div class="relative py-4 pl-4 font-mono text-sm md:text-sm overflow-x-auto code-block-scroll" tabindex="0"><pre class="inline-block min-w-full pr-4"><code>&lt;frontend_aesthetics&gt;
You tend to converge toward generic, &quot;on distribution&quot; outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the &quot;AI slop&quot; aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.

Focus on:
- Typography: Choose fonts that are beautiful, unique, and interesting. Avoid generic fonts like Arial and Inter; opt instead for distinctive choices that elevate the frontend&#x27;s aesthetics.
- Color &amp; Theme: Commit to a cohesive aesthetic. Use CSS variables for consistency. Dominant colors with sharp accents outperform timid, evenly-distributed palettes. Draw from IDE themes and cultural aesthetics for inspiration.
- Motion: Use animations for effects and micro-interactions. Prioritize CSS-only solutions for HTML. Use Motion library for React when available. Focus on high-impact moments: one well-orchestrated page load with staggered reveals (animation-delay) creates more delight than scattered micro-interactions.
- Backgrounds: Create atmosphere and depth rather than defaulting to solid colors. Layer CSS gradients, use geometric patterns, or add contextual effects that match the overall aesthetic.

Avoid generic AI-generated aesthetics:
- Overused font families (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system fonts)
- Clichéd color schemes (particularly purple gradients on white backgrounds)
- Predictable layouts and component patterns
- Cookie-cutter design that lacks context-specific character

Interpret creatively and make unexpected choices that feel genuinely designed for the context. Vary between light and dark themes, different fonts, different aesthetics. You still tend to converge on common choices (Space Grotesk, for example) across generations. Avoid this: it is critical that you think outside the box!
&lt;/frontend_aesthetics&gt;</code></pre></div></div>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">You can also refer to the <a class="inline-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/frontend-design/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md">full skill definition</a>.</p>
<h2 class="font-heading leading-[1.25] text-text-100"><div class="group relative pt-6 pb-2" id="migration-considerations"><div class="absolute hidden lg:flex lg:items-center lg:-ml-8 top-[calc(1.5rem+0.175em)]"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0
  can-focus
  select-none
  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:shadow-none
  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 hover:text-text-100 Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy link to clipboard" title="Copy link to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="inline" style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="inline" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.623 9.648a.5.5 0 0 1 .648.281l1.008 2.559a4 4 0 0 1-7.363 3.122l-.08-.19-1.008-2.558a.5.5 0 0 1 .93-.367l1.008 2.558a3 3 0 1 0 5.583-2.198l-1.008-2.559a.5.5 0 0 1 .282-.648M8.9 7.208a.5.5 0 0 1 .649.283l1.833 4.652a.5.5 0 0 1-.931.366L8.619 7.857a.5.5 0 0 1 .281-.649M7.17 2.253a4 4 0 0 1 4.994 2.327l1.008 2.558a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366l-1.009-2.558a3 3 0 0 0-5.582 2.199L6.66 9.704a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366L4.72 7.51a4 4 0 0 1 2.255-5.187z"></path></svg></div></button></div><div>Migration considerations</div><div class="absolute top-0 -right-6 hidden lg:flex"></div></div></h2>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">When migrating to Claude 4.6 models from earlier generations:</p>
<ol class="list-none pl-8 [counter-reset:item] [&amp;_ul]:pl-8 [&amp;_ol]:pl-8 [&amp;_ol]:[counter-reset:item] mb-4">
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]">
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">Be specific about desired behavior</strong>: Consider describing exactly what you&#x27;d like to see in the output.</p>
</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]">
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">Frame your instructions with modifiers</strong>: Adding modifiers that encourage Claude to increase the quality and detail of its output can help better shape Claude&#x27;s performance. For example, instead of &quot;Create an analytics dashboard&quot;, use &quot;Create an analytics dashboard. Include as many relevant features and interactions as possible. Go beyond the basics to create a fully-featured implementation.&quot;</p>
</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]">
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">Request specific features explicitly</strong>: Animations and interactive elements should be requested explicitly when desired.</p>
</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]">
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">Update thinking configuration</strong>: Claude 4.6 models use <a class="inline-link" href="/docs/en/build-with-claude/adaptive-thinking">adaptive thinking</a> (<code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">thinking: {type: &quot;adaptive&quot;}</code>) instead of manual thinking with <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">budget_tokens</code>. Use the <a class="inline-link" href="/docs/en/build-with-claude/effort">effort parameter</a> to control thinking depth.</p>
</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]">
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">Migrate away from prefilled responses</strong>: Prefilled responses on the last assistant turn are deprecated starting with Claude 4.6 models. See <a class="inline-link" href="#migrating-away-from-prefilled-responses">Migrating away from prefilled responses</a> for detailed guidance on alternatives.</p>
</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]">
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">Tune anti-laziness prompting</strong>: If your prompts previously encouraged the model to be more thorough or use tools more aggressively, dial back that guidance. Claude 4.6 models are significantly more proactive and may overtrigger on instructions that were needed for previous models.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">For detailed migration steps, see the <a class="inline-link" href="/docs/en/about-claude/models/migration-guide">Migration guide</a>.</p>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100"><div class="group relative pt-6 pb-2" id="migrating-from-claude-sonnet-4-5-to-claude-sonnet-4-6"><div class="absolute hidden lg:flex lg:items-center lg:-ml-8 top-[calc(1.5rem+0.08em)]"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0
  can-focus
  select-none
  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:shadow-none
  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 hover:text-text-100 Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy link to clipboard" title="Copy link to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="inline" style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="inline" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.623 9.648a.5.5 0 0 1 .648.281l1.008 2.559a4 4 0 0 1-7.363 3.122l-.08-.19-1.008-2.558a.5.5 0 0 1 .93-.367l1.008 2.558a3 3 0 1 0 5.583-2.198l-1.008-2.559a.5.5 0 0 1 .282-.648M8.9 7.208a.5.5 0 0 1 .649.283l1.833 4.652a.5.5 0 0 1-.931.366L8.619 7.857a.5.5 0 0 1 .281-.649M7.17 2.253a4 4 0 0 1 4.994 2.327l1.008 2.558a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366l-1.009-2.558a3 3 0 0 0-5.582 2.199L6.66 9.704a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366L4.72 7.51a4 4 0 0 1 2.255-5.187z"></path></svg></div></button></div><div>Migrating from Claude Sonnet 4.5 to Claude Sonnet 4.6</div><div class="absolute top-0 -right-6 hidden lg:flex"></div></div></h3>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">Claude Sonnet 4.6 defaults to an effort level of <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">high</code>, in contrast to Claude Sonnet 4.5 which had no effort parameter. Consider adjusting the effort parameter as you migrate from Claude Sonnet 4.5 to Claude Sonnet 4.6. If not explicitly set, you may experience higher latency with the default effort level.</p>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">Recommended effort settings:</strong></p>
<ul class="list-none pl-8 [&amp;_ul]:pl-8 [&amp;_ol]:pl-8 mb-4">
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]"><strong class="font-semibold">Medium</strong> for most applications</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]"><strong class="font-semibold">Low</strong> for high-volume or latency-sensitive workloads</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]">Set a large max output token budget (64k tokens recommended) at medium or high effort to give the model room to think and act</li>
</ul>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">When to use Opus 4.7 instead:</strong> For the hardest, longest-horizon problems (large-scale code migrations, deep research, extended autonomous work), Opus 4.7 remains the right choice. Sonnet 4.6 is optimized for workloads where fast turnaround and cost efficiency matter most.</p>
<h4 class="text-lg font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100"><div class="group relative pt-6 pb-2" id="if-you-re-not-using-extended-thinking"><div class="absolute hidden lg:flex lg:items-center lg:-ml-8 top-[calc(1.5rem+0.05em)]"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0
  can-focus
  select-none
  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:shadow-none
  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 hover:text-text-100 Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy link to clipboard" title="Copy link to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="inline" style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="inline" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.623 9.648a.5.5 0 0 1 .648.281l1.008 2.559a4 4 0 0 1-7.363 3.122l-.08-.19-1.008-2.558a.5.5 0 0 1 .93-.367l1.008 2.558a3 3 0 1 0 5.583-2.198l-1.008-2.559a.5.5 0 0 1 .282-.648M8.9 7.208a.5.5 0 0 1 .649.283l1.833 4.652a.5.5 0 0 1-.931.366L8.619 7.857a.5.5 0 0 1 .281-.649M7.17 2.253a4 4 0 0 1 4.994 2.327l1.008 2.558a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366l-1.009-2.558a3 3 0 0 0-5.582 2.199L6.66 9.704a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366L4.72 7.51a4 4 0 0 1 2.255-5.187z"></path></svg></div></button></div><div>If you&#x27;re not using extended thinking</div><div class="absolute top-0 -right-6 hidden lg:flex"></div></div></h4>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">If you&#x27;re not using extended thinking on Claude Sonnet 4.5, you can continue without it on Claude Sonnet 4.6. You should explicitly set effort to the level appropriate for your use case. At <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">low</code> effort with thinking disabled, you can expect similar or better performance relative to Claude Sonnet 4.5 with no extended thinking.</p>
<div class="group relative my-6 rounded-lg bg-bg-000 overflow-hidden border-0.5 border-border-300"><div class="group flex items-center justify-between border-b-0.5 border-border-300 px-4 py-1"><div class="text-xs py-1 flex items-center gap-2 text-text-500">Python</div><div class="flex items-center gap-1"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0

  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50
  disabled:shadow-none
  disabled:drop-shadow-none border-transparent
          transition
          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy code"><div class="relative flex items-center justify-center"><div class="transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-100" style="width:14px;height:14px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-100" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M12.5 3A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 14 4.5V6h1.5A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 17 7.5v8a1.5 1.5 0 0 1-1.5 1.5h-8A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 6 15.5V14H4.5A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 3 12.5v-8A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 4.5 3zm1.5 9.5a1.5 1.5 0 0 1-1.5 1.5H7v1.5a.5.5 0 0 0 .5.5h8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5-.5v-8a.5.5 0 0 0-.5-.5H14zM4.5 4a.5.5 0 0 0-.5.5v8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5.5h8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5-.5v-8a.5.5 0 0 0-.5-.5z"></path></svg></div><div class="absolute transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-0" style="width:14px;height:14px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="absolute transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 opacity-0" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M15.188 5.11a.5.5 0 0 1 .752.626l-.056.084-7.5 9a.5.5 0 0 1-.738.033l-3.5-3.5-.064-.078a.501.501 0 0 1 .693-.693l.078.064 3.113 3.113 7.15-8.58z"></path></svg></div></div></button></div></div><div class="relative py-4 pl-4 font-mono text-sm md:text-sm overflow-x-auto code-block-scroll" tabindex="0"><div class="inline-block min-w-full pr-4"><pre class="shiki shiki-themes github-light-default dark-plus" style="--shiki-light:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6;--shiki-light-bg:transparent;--shiki-dark-bg:transparent" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">client.messages.create(</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#953800;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">    model</span><span style="--shiki-light:#CF222E;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">=</span><span style="--shiki-light:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">"claude-sonnet-4-6"</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#953800;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">    max_tokens</span><span style="--shiki-light:#CF222E;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">=</span><span style="--shiki-light:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#B5CEA8">8192</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#953800;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">    thinking</span><span style="--shiki-light:#CF222E;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">=</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">{</span><span style="--shiki-light:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">"type"</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">: </span><span style="--shiki-light:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">"disabled"</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">},</span></span>

<span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">)</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div>
<h4 class="text-lg font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100"><div class="group relative pt-6 pb-2" id="if-you-re-using-extended-thinking"><div class="absolute hidden lg:flex lg:items-center lg:-ml-8 top-[calc(1.5rem+0.05em)]"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center
  relative
  isolate
  shrink-0
  can-focus
  select-none
  disabled:pointer-events-none
  disabled:opacity-50

          font-base
          duration-300
          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-6 w-6 rounded-md opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200 text-text-500 hover:text-text-100 Button_fill__JiIcX Button_ghost__BUAoh" type="button" aria-label="Copy link to clipboard" title="Copy link to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="inline" style="width:20px;height:20px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="flex-shrink:0" class="inline" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.623 9.648a.5.5 0 0 1 .648.281l1.008 2.559a4 4 0 0 1-7.363 3.122l-.08-.19-1.008-2.558a.5.5 0 0 1 .93-.367l1.008 2.558a3 3 0 1 0 5.583-2.198l-1.008-2.559a.5.5 0 0 1 .282-.648M8.9 7.208a.5.5 0 0 1 .649.283l1.833 4.652a.5.5 0 0 1-.931.366L8.619 7.857a.5.5 0 0 1 .281-.649M7.17 2.253a4 4 0 0 1 4.994 2.327l1.008 2.558a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366l-1.009-2.558a3 3 0 0 0-5.582 2.199L6.66 9.704a.5.5 0 0 1-.93.366L4.72 7.51a4 4 0 0 1 2.255-5.187z"></path></svg></div></button></div><div>If you&#x27;re using extended thinking</div><div class="absolute top-0 -right-6 hidden lg:flex"></div></div></h4>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">If you&#x27;re using extended thinking with <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">budget_tokens</code> on Claude Sonnet 4.5, it is still functional on Claude Sonnet 4.6 but is deprecated. Migrate to <a class="inline-link" href="/docs/en/build-with-claude/adaptive-thinking">adaptive thinking</a> with the <a class="inline-link" href="/docs/en/build-with-claude/effort">effort parameter</a>.</p>
<h5>Migrating to adaptive thinking</h5>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">Adaptive thinking is particularly well suited to the following workload patterns:</p>
<ul class="list-none pl-8 [&amp;_ul]:pl-8 [&amp;_ol]:pl-8 mb-4">
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]"><strong class="font-semibold">Autonomous multi-step agents:</strong> coding agents that turn requirements into working software, data analysis pipelines, and bug finding where the model runs independently across many steps. Adaptive thinking lets the model calibrate its reasoning per step, staying on path over longer trajectories. For these workloads, start at <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">high</code> effort. If latency or token usage is a concern, scale down to <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">medium</code>.</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]"><strong class="font-semibold">Computer use agents:</strong> Claude Sonnet 4.6 achieved best-in-class accuracy on computer use evaluations using adaptive mode.</li>
<li class="mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[&#x27;•&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:before:content-[counter(item)&#x27;.&#x27;] [ol&gt;&amp;]:[counter-increment:item]"><strong class="font-semibold">Bimodal workloads:</strong> a mix of easy and hard tasks where adaptive skips thinking on simple queries and reasons deeply on complex ones.</li>
</ul>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">When using adaptive thinking, evaluate <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">medium</code> and <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">high</code> effort on your tasks. The right level depends on your workload&#x27;s tradeoff between quality, latency, and token usage.</p>

<span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#953800;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">    max_tokens</span><span style="--shiki-light:#CF222E;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">=</span><span style="--shiki-light:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#B5CEA8">64000</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">,</span></span>

<span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#953800;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">    messages</span><span style="--shiki-light:#CF222E;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">=</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">[{</span><span style="--shiki-light:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">"role"</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">: </span><span style="--shiki-light:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">"user"</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">, </span><span style="--shiki-light:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">"content"</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">: </span><span style="--shiki-light:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">"..."</span><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">}],</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="--shiki-light:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#f3f7f6">)</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div>
<h5>Keeping budget_tokens during migration</h5>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0">If you need to keep <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">budget_tokens</code> temporarily while migrating, a budget around 16k tokens provides headroom for harder problems without risk of runaway token usage. This configuration is deprecated and will be removed in a future model release.</p>
<p class="mb-2 last:mb-0"><strong class="font-semibold">For coding use cases</strong> (agentic coding, tool-heavy workflows, code generation), start with <code class="relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone">medium</code> effort:</p>

If the problem persists \u003clink\u003econtact support\u003c/link\u003e for assistance.\",\"zzjuAqkFTh\":\"e.g., Focus on debugging patterns and error handling\"},\"hideErrors\":true,\"children\":\"$L3a\"}]}]}]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"20:[[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"This is the single reference for prompt engineering with Claude's latest models, including Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and Claude Haiku 4.5. It covers foundational techniques, output control, tool use, thinking, and agentic systems. Jump to the section that matches your situation.\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"$L3c\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"For an overview of model capabilities, see the \",[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"models overview\"}],\". For details on what's new in Claude Opus 4.7, see \",[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"/docs/en/about-claude/models/whats-new-claude-4-7\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"What's new in Claude Opus 4.7\"}],\". For migration guidance, see the \",[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"/docs/en/about-claude/models/migration-guide\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"Migration guide\"}],\".\"]}]}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"font-heading leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"prompting-claude-opus-4-7\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":2,\"children\":\"Prompting Claude Opus 4.7\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude Opus 4.7 is our most capable generally available model, with particular strengths in long-horizon agentic work, knowledge work, vision, and memory tasks. It performs well out of the box on existing Claude Opus 4.6 prompts. The patterns below cover the behaviors that most often require tuning.\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"$L3f\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"For API parameter changes when migrating from Claude Opus 4.6 (effort levels, task budgets, thinking configuration, sampling-parameter removal, and tokenization), see the \",[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"/docs/en/about-claude/models/migration-guide#migrating-to-claude-opus-4-7\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"migration guide\"}],\".\"]}]}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"response-length-and-verbosity\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Response length and verbosity\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude Opus 4.7 calibrates response length to how complex it judges the task to be, rather than defaulting to a fixed verbosity. This usually means shorter answers on simple lookups and much longer ones on open-ended analysis.\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"If your product depends on a certain style or verbosity of output, you may need to tune your prompts. As an example, to decrease verbosity, you might add:\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Provide concise, focused responses. Skip non-essential context, and keep examples minimal.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"If you see specific examples of kinds of verbosity (i.e. over-explaining), you can add additional instructions in your prompt to prevent them. Positive examples showing how Claude can communicate with the appropriate level of concision tend to be more effective than negative examples or instructions that tell the model what not to do.\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"calibrating-effort-and-thinking-depth\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Calibrating effort and thinking Experiment with other effort levels to further tune token usage and intelligence:\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"42:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"list\",\"className\":\"mb-4\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"className\":\"list-none pl-8 [\u0026_ul]:pl-8 [\u0026_ol]:pl-8\",\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul\u003e\u0026]:before:content-['•'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:before:content-[counter(item)'.'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:[counter-increment:item]\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"max\"}],\":\"]}],\" Max effort can deliver performance gains in some use cases, but may show diminishing returns from increased token usage. This setting can also sometimes be prone to overthinking. We recommend testing max effort for intelligence-demanding tasks.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul\u003e\u0026]:before:content-['•'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:before:content-[counter(item)'.'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:[counter-increment:item]\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"xhigh\"}],\" (new):\"]}],\" Extra high effort is the best setting for most coding and agentic use cases.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul\u003e\u0026]:before:content-['•'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:before:content-[counter(item)'.'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:[counter-increment:item]\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"high\"}],\":\"]}],\" This setting balances token usage and intelligence. For most intelligence-sensitive use cases, we recommend a minimum of \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"high\"}],\" effort.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul\u003e\u0026]:before:content-['•'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:before:content-[counter(item)'.'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:[counter-increment:item]\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"medium\"}],\":\"]}],\" Good for cost-sensitive use cases that need to reduce token usage while trading off intelligence.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0 relative before:absolute before:left-[-2rem] before:w-6 before:text-center [ul\u003e\u0026]:before:content-['•'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:before:content-[counter(item)'.'] [ol\u003e\u0026]:[counter-increment:item]\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"low\"}],\":\"]}],\" Reserve for short, scoped tasks and latency-sensitive workloads that are not intelligence-sensitive.\"]}],\"\\n\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"43:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Meaningfully changing from Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Opus 4.7 respects effort levels strictly, especially at the low end. At \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"low\"}],\" and \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"medium\"}],\", the model scopes its work to what was asked rather than going above and beyond. This is good for latency and cost, but on moderately complex tasks running at \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"low\"}],\" effort there is some risk of under-thinking.\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"44:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"If you observe shallow reasoning on complex problems, raise effort to \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"high\"}],\" or \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"xhigh\"}],\" rather than prompting around it. If you need to keep effort at \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"low\"}],\" for latency, add targeted guidance:\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"45:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"This task involves multi-step reasoning. Think carefully through the problem before responding.\"}]\n46:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"We expect effort to be more important for this model than for any prior Opus, and recommend experimenting with it actively when you upgrade.\"}]}]\n47:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"The triggering behavior for adaptive thinking is steerable. If you find the model thinking more often than you'd like — which can happen with large or complex system prompts — add guidance to steer it. As always, measure the effect of any prompting changes on performance. Example:\"}]}]\n48:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Thinking adds latency and should only be used when it will meaningfully improve answer quality — typically for problems that require multi-step reasoning. When in doubt, respond directly.\"}]\n49:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Conversely, if you're running hard workloads at \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"medium\"}],\" and seeing under-thinking, the first lever is to raise effort. If you need finer control, prompt for it directly.\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"4a:[\"$\",\"$L3f\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"If you are running Claude Opus 4.7 at \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"max\"}],\" or \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"xhigh\"}],\" effort, set a large max output token budget so the model has room to think and act across its subagents and tool calls. We recommend starting at 64k tokens and tuning from there.\"]}]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"4b:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"tool-use-triggering\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Tool use triggering\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"4c:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Claude Opus 4.7 has a tendency to use tools less often than Claude Opus 4.6 and to use reasoning more. This produces better results in most cases. However, increasing the effort setting is a useful lever to increase the level of tool usage, especially in knowledge work. \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"high\"}],\" or \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"xhigh\"}],\" effort settings show substantially more tool usage in agentic search and coding. For scenarios where you want more tool use, you can also adjust your prompt to explicitly instruct the model about when and how to properly use its tools. For instance, if you find that the model is not using your web search tools, clearly describe why and how it should.\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"4d:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"user-facing-progress-updates\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"User-facing progress updates\"}]}]\n4e:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude Opus 4.7 provides more regular, higher-quality updates to the user throughout long agentic traces. If you've added scaffolding to force interim status messages (\\\"After every 3 tool calls, summarize progress\\\"), try removing it. If you find that the length or contents of Claude Opus 4.7's user-facing updates are not well-calibrated to your use case, explicitly describe what these updates should look like in the prompt and provide examples.\"}]}]\n4f:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"more-literal-instruction-following\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"More literal instruction following\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"50:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude Opus 4.7 interprets prompts more literally and explicitly than Claude Opus 4.6, particularly at lower effort levels. It will not silently generalize an instruction from one item to another, and it will not infer requests you didn't make. The upside of this literalism is precision and less thrash, and it generally performs better for API use cases with carefully tuned prompts, structured extraction, and pipelines where you want predictable behavior. If you need Claude to apply an instruction broadly, state the scope explicitly (for example, \\\"Apply this formatting to every section, not just the first one\\\").\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"51:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"tone-and-writing-style\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Tone and writing style\"}]}]\n52:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"As with any new model, prose style on long-form writing may shift. Claude Opus 4.7 is more direct and opinionated, with less validation-forward phrasing and fewer emoji than Claude Opus 4.6's warmer style. If your product relies on a specific voice, re-evaluate style prompts against the new baseline.\"}]}]\n53:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"For instance, if your product voice is warmer or more conversational, add:\"}]}]\n54:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Use a warm, collaborative tone. Acknowledge the user's framing before answering.\"}]\n55:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"controlling-subagent-spawning\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Controlling subagent spawning\"}]}]\n56:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude Opus 4.7 tends to spawn fewer subagents by default. However, this behavior is steerable through prompting; give Claude Opus 4.7 explicit guidance around when subagents are desirable. A toy example for a coding use case:\"}]}]\n57:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Do not spawn a subagent for work you can complete directly in a single response (e.g. refactoring a function you can already see).\\n\\nSpawn multiple subagents in the same turn when fanning out across items or reading multiple files.\"}]\n58:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"design-and-frontend-defaults\",\"className\":\"pt-6"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1," pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Design and frontend defaults\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"59:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Claude Opus 4.7 has stronger design instincts than Claude Opus 4.6, with a consistent default house style: warm cream/off-white backgrounds (~\",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"#F4F1EA\"}],\"), serif display type (Georgia, Fraunces, Playfair), italic word-accents, and a terracotta/amber accent. This reads well for editorial, hospitality, and portfolio briefs, but will feel off for dashboards, dev tools, fintech, healthcare, or enterprise apps — and it appears in slide decks as well as web UIs.\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"5a:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"This default is persistent. Generic instructions (\\\"don't use cream,\\\" \\\"make it clean and minimal\\\") tend to shift the model to a different fixed palette rather than producing variety. Two approaches work reliably:\"}]}]\n5b:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":\"1. Specify a concrete alternative.\"}],\" The model follows explicit specs precisely:\"]}]}]\n132:T6c5,"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"Design a desktop landing page for a supplement brand called AEFRM.\n\nThe visual direction should come from a cold monochrome atmosphere using pale silver-gray tones that gradually deepen into blue-gray and near-black, similar to a misted metallic surface.\n\nThe page should feel sharp and controlled, with a strong sense of structure and restraint.\n\nUse this tonal system across the full page instead of introducing bright accent colors.\n\nUse the uploaded image on the hero design in black and white.\n\nThe layout should be built with clear horizontal sections and a centered max-width container. Use 4px corner radius consistently across cards, buttons, inputs, and media frames. Margins should feel generous, with enough empty space around each section so the page breathes.\n\nTypography should use a square, angular sans-serif with wider letter spacing than usual, especially in headings and navigation, so the text feels more engineered and less compressed. Headline text can be large and uppercase, while supporting copy remains short and sparse. The sub texts should be written with Alumni Sans SC in 4-6px like tiny little texts on corners bottom centre like that.\n\nFor the structure, start with a hero section containing a strong product statement, one short supporting paragraph, and a clean product placeholder or packshot frame. Below that, add a benefit grid with three or four blocks, then a formulation or ingredients section, and finally a cta.\n\nButtons should be flat and precise, with subtle hover changes using transition: all 160ms ease out where brightness and border contrast shift slightly rather than using dramatic motion.\n\nColor palette should stay within this range:\n#E9ECEC, #C9D2D4, #8C9A9E, #44545B, #11171B."])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"5c:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"$132\"}]\n5d:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":\"2. Have the model propose options before building.\"}],\" This breaks the default and gives users control. If you previously relied on \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"temperature\"}],\" for design variety, use this approach — it produces meaningfully different directions across runs. Example prompt:\"]}]}]\n5e:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Before building, propose 4 distinct visual directions tailored to this brief (each as: bg hex / accent hex / typeface — one-line rationale). Ask the user to pick one, then implement only that direction.\"}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"5f:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Additionally, Claude Opus 4.7 requires less frontend design prompting than previous models to avoid generic patterns that users call the \\\"AI slop\\\" aesthetic. With earlier models, we recommended a lengthier prompt snippet in our \",[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/frontend-design/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"frontend-design skill\"}],\". However, Claude Opus 4.7 generates distinctive, creative frontends with more minimal prompting guidance. This prompt snippet works well with the above prompting advice for variety:\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"60:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"\u003cfrontend_aesthetics\u003e\\nNEVER use generic AI-generated aesthetics like overused font families (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system fonts), cliched color schemes (particularly purple gradients on white or dark backgrounds), predictable layouts and component patterns, and cookie-cutter design that lacks context-specific character. Use unique fonts, cohesive colors and themes, and animations for effects and micro-interactions.\\n\u003c/frontend_aesthetics\u003e\"}]\n61:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"interactive-coding-products\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Interactive coding products\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"62:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Claude Opus 4.7's token usage and behavior can differ between autonomous, asynchronous coding agents with a single user turn and interactive, synchronous coding agents with multiple user turns. Specifically, it tends to use more tokens in interactive settings, primarily because it reasons more after user turns. This can improve long-horizon coherence, instruction following, and coding capabilities in long, interactive coding sessions, but also comes with more token usage. To maximize both performance and token efficiency in coding products, we recommend using \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"xhigh\"}],\" or \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"high\"}],\" effort, adding autonomous features like an auto mode, and reducing the number of human interactions required from your users.\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"63:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Of course, when limiting the number of required user interactions, it's important to specify the task, intent, and relevant constraints upfront in the first human turn. Providing well-specified, clear, and accurate task descriptions upfront can help maximize autonomy and intelligence while minimizing extra token usage after user turns. We find that because Claude Opus 4.7 is more autonomous than prior models, this usage pattern helps to maximize performance. In contrast, ambiguous or underspecified prompts conveyed progressively over multiple user turns tend to relatively reduce token efficiency and sometimes performance.\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"64:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"code-review-harnesses\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Code review harnesses\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"65:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude Opus 4.7 is meaningfully better at finding bugs than prior models, and has both higher recall and precision in our evals — 11pp better recall in one of our hardest bug-finding evals based on real Anthropic PRs. However, if your code-review harness was tuned for an earlier model, you may initially see lower recall. This is likely a harness effect, not a capability regression. When a review prompt says things like \\\"only report high-severity issues,\\\" \\\"be conservative,\\\" or \\\"don't nitpick,\\\" Claude Opus 4.7 may follow that instruction more faithfully than earlier models did — it may investigate the code just as thoroughly, identify the bugs, and then not report findings it judges to be below your stated bar. This can show up as the model doing the same depth of investigation but converting fewer investigations into reported findings, especially on lower-severity bugs. Precision typically rises, but measured recall can fall even though the model's underlying bug-finding ability has improved.\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"66:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Some recommended prompt language:\"}]}]\n67:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Report every issue you find, including ones you are uncertain about or consider low-severity. Do not filter for importance or confidence at this stage - a separate verification step will do that. Your goal here is coverage: it is better to surface a finding that later gets filtered out than to silently drop a real bug. For each finding, include your confidence level and an estimated severity so a downstream filter can rank them.\"}]\n68:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"This prompt can be used without having an actual second step, but moving confidence filtering out of the finding step often helps. If your harness has a separate verification, deduplication, or ranking stage, tell the model explicitly that its job at the finding stage is coverage rather than filtering.\"}]}]\n69:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"If you do want the model to self-filter in a single pass, be concrete about where the bar is rather than using qualitative terms like \\\"important\\\" — for example, \\\"report any bugs that could cause incorrect behavior, a test failure, or a misleading result; only omit nits like pure style or naming preferences.\\\"\"}]}]\n6a:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"We recommend iterating on prompts against a subset of your evals or test cases to validate recall or F1 score gains.\"}]}]\n6b:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"computer-use\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Computer use\"}]}]\n6c:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLen"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"gth\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/computer-use-tool\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"Computer use\"}],\" capability works across resolutions, up to a new maximum resolution of 2576px / 3.75MP. Use standard paragraph breaks for organization and reserve markdown primarily for `inline code`, code blocks (```...```), and simple headings (###, and ###). Avoid using **bold** and *italics*.\n\nDO NOT use ordered lists (1. ...) or unordered lists (*) unless : a) you're presenting truly discrete items where a list format is the best option, or b) the user explicitly requests a list or ranking\n\nInstead of listing items with bullets or numbers, incorporate them naturally into sentences. This guidance applies especially to technical writing. Using prose instead of excessive formatting will improve user satisfaction. NEVER output a series of overly short bullet points.\n\nYour goal is readable, flowing text that guides the reader naturally through ideas rather than fragmenting information into isolated points.\n\u003c/avoid_excessive_markdown_and_bullet_points\u003e"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"96:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"$138\",\"label\":\"Sample prompt to minimize markdown\"}]\n97:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"la-te-x-output\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"LaTeX output\"}]}]\n98:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude Opus 4.6 defaults to LaTeX for mathematical expressions, equations, and technical explanations. If you prefer plain text, add the following instructions to your prompt:\"}]}]\n99:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Format your response in plain text only. Do not use LaTeX, MathJax, or any markup notation such as \\\\( \\\\), $, or \\\\frac{}{}. Write all math expressions using standard text characters (e.g., \\\"/\\\" for division, \\\"*\\\" for multiplication, and \\\"^\\\" for exponents).\",\"label\":\"Sample prompt\"}]\n9a:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"document-creation\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Document creation\"}]}]\n9b:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude's latest models excel at creating presentations, animations, and visual documents with impressive creative flair and strong instruction following. The models produce polished, usable output on the first try in most cases.\"}]}]\n9c:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"For best results with document creation:\"}]}]\n9d:[\"$\",\"$L40\",null,{\"language\":\"text\",\"code\":\"Create a professional presentation on [topic]. Include thoughtful design elements, visual hierarchy, and engaging animations where appropriate.\",\"label\":\"Sample prompt\"}]\n9e:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"migrat"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"ing-away-from-prefilled-responses\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Migrating away from prefilled responses\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"9f:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Starting with Claude 4.6 models and \",[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"https://anthropic.com/glasswing\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"Claude Mythos Preview\"}],\", prefilled responses on the last assistant turn are no longer supported. On Mythos Preview, requests with prefilled assistant messages return a 400 error. Model intelligence and instruction following has advanced such that most use cases of prefill no longer require it. Existing models will continue to support prefills, and adding assistant messages elsewhere in the conversation is not affected.\"]}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"a0:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Here are common prefill scenarios and how to migrate away from them:\"}]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"a1:[\"$\",\"$L133\",null,{\"title\":\"Controlling output formatting\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Prefills have been used to force specific output formats like JSON/YAML, classification, and similar patterns where the prefill constrains Claude to a particular structure.\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":\"Migration:\"}],\" The \",[\"$\",\"$L3d\",null,{\"href\":\"/docs/en/build-with-claude/structured-outputs\",\"className\":\"inline-link\",\"children\":\"Structured Outputs\"}],\" feature is designed specifically to constrain Claude's responses to follow a given schema. Try simply asking the model to conform to your output structure first, as newer models can reliably match complex schemas when told to, especially if implemented with retries. For classification tasks, use either tools with an enum field containing your valid labels or structured outputs.\"]}]}]]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"a2:[\"$\",\"$L133\",null,{\"title\":\"Eliminating preambles\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Prefills like \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"Here is the requested summary:\\\\n\"}],\" were used to skip introductory text.\"]}]}],[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":\"Migration:\"}],\" Use direct instructions in the system prompt: \\\"Respond directly without preamble. Do not start with phrases like 'Here is...', 'Based on...', etc.\\\" Alternatively, direct the model to output within XML tags, use structured outputs, or use tool calling. If the occasional preamble slips through, strip it in post-processing.\"]}]}]]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"a3:[\"$\",\"$L133\",null,{\"title\":\"Avoiding bad refusals\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Prefills were used to steer around unnecessary refusals.\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":\"Migration:\"}],\" Claude is much better at appropriate refusals now. Clear prompting within the \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"relative inline bg-bg-300 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono break-words box-decoration-clone\",\"children\":\"user\"}],\" message without prefill should be sufficient.\"]}]}]]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"a4:[\"$\",\"$L133\",null,{\"title\":\"Continuations\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Prefills were used to continue partial completions, resume interrupted responses, or pick up where a previous generation left off.\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":\"Migration:\"}],\" Move the continuation to the user message, and include the final text from the interrupted response: \\\"Your previous response was interrupted and ended with `[previous_response]`. Continue from where you left off.\\\" If this is part of error-handling or incomplete-response-handling and there is no UX penalty, retry the request.\"]}]}]]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"a5:[\"$\",\"$L133\",null,{\"title\":\"Context hydration and role consistency\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Prefills were used to periodically ensure refreshed or injected context.\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"className\":\"font-semibold\",\"children\":\"Migration:\"}],\" For very long conversations, inject what were previously prefilled-assistant reminders into the user turn. If context hydration is part of a more complex agentic system, consider hydrating via tools (expose or encourage use of tools containing context based on heuristics such as number of turns) or during context compaction.\"]}]}]]}]\n"])</script><script nonce="qBEX3dx3rutX15Giyr7d5Q==">self.__next_f.push([1,"a6:[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"font-heading leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"tool-use\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":2,\"children\":\"Tool use\"}]}]\na7:[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xl font-medium leading-[1.25] text-text-100\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L3e\",null,{\"slug\":\"tool-usage\",\"className\":\"pt-6 pb-2\",\"headingLevel\":3,\"children\":\"Tool usage\"}]}]\na8:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude's latest models are trained for precise instruction following and benefit from explicit direction to use specific tools. intended.\"}]}]\na9:[\"$\",\"$L3b\",null,{\"contentType\":\"paragraph\",\"minLength\":20,\"className\":\"mb-2 last:mb-0\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"For Claude to take action, be
